comment: Concrete ADR set with all universal-shape fields populated + cost-curve on stack ADRs. Used by tests/i2p/test_adr_shape.py to lock the shape contract.

adrs.title: Use NextJS for the frontend
adrs.context: Solo founder, 2-week prototype window, marketing landing pages need SEO.
adrs.decision: OPT-A NextJS App Router with React Server Components.
adrs.consequences: Mature ecosystem; Vercel hosting tightly integrated; lock-in to React.

options_considered.name: Vite + React
options_considered.rationale_for: Faster dev loop; no SSR coupling.
options_considered.rationale_against: No SSR built-in; SEO needs custom plumbing.

adrs.falsification_signal: If marketing pivots to API-first GTM (no landing pages), revisit within 30 days.

adrs.title: Use PostgreSQL with TimescaleDB
adrs.context: Need to store hourly price snapshots for ~50k SKUs; 36-month retention.
adrs.decision: OPT-B Postgres + TimescaleDB self-hosted on TR-region VPS.
adrs.consequences: Continuous aggregates make 30-day chart sub-100ms; self-host = solo-founder ops burden.

options_considered.name: PostgreSQL plain
options_considered.rationale_against: Index bloat at 1B+ rows; manual partitioning.

options_considered.name: PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB
options_considered.rationale_against: Supabase doesn't support TimescaleDB on free tier.

adrs.falsification_signal: Active SKU > 200 by week 4 OR 30-day chart query p95 > 800ms.

register_md:
# ADR Register

- ADR-2026-05-10-001 — Use NextJS for the frontend — status:accepted
- ADR-2026-05-10-002 — Use PostgreSQL with TimescaleDB — status:accepted
